//: job-scout platform v4.0.0 — zero-prompt permissions for unattended AND interactive scans (any profile).
//install: On the machine that runs the scans: copy this file to .claude/settings.json at the REPO ROOT (mkdir -p .claude && cp claude-settings.example.json .claude/settings.json). Shipped as an example rather than live config so cloning the repo never silently grants permissions. Re-copy after engine updates. Your own 'Yes, don't ask again' clicks accumulate separately in .claude/settings.local.json and are preserved.
//scope: Scoped to the scan workflow: core script execution (scripts do their own outbound HTTP), read-only shell inspection of results, git operations for state_sync, WebFetch/WebSearch for the judgment layer's occasional direct look at a posting, state writes under profiles/*/state only. No blanket skip-permissions flag; the cron fallback (claude -p) may additionally need --allowedTools set to the same list — a broader flag like --dangerously-skip-permissions trades safety for convenience and is NOT the default.
